data.title: Tyra raised to buy by BofA ahead of TYRA-300 data readout
data.description:
• Tyra Biosciences (NASDAQ:TYRA) has been upgraded to buy by BofA ahead of an expected data readout next week from a Phase 1/2 study of its FGFR3-inhibitor TYRA-300 for urothelial carcinoma and solid tumors.
  • BofA said that while Johnson & Johnson’s (JNJ) FGF erdafitinib set the standard for efficacy, “poor tolerability has made it largely a non-starter,” making safety vs. efficacy “the bar for success for ‘300.”
  • “After conferring with our docs, we see the potential for lower rates of hyperphosphatemia, where levels on par with Relay’s (RLAY) FGFR2 ‘4008 would be a clear win,” BofA said in its note.
  • BofA added that “despite recent strength, we still see potential upside for TYRA (TYRA) shares on a positive readout.”
  • The investment bank raised its price target of the stock to $31 From $22.

data.title: Vistra in 'catbird seat' among U.S. independent power producers, JPM analyst says
data.description:
Vistra (NYSE:VST) regains its usual spot near the top of the S&P 500 leaderboard, +3.7% in Friday's trading after more than quadrupling during the past year as the S&P's best performer.

Vistra (VST) is "in the catbird seat," as the top pick among U.S. independent power producers, according to J.P. Morgan's Jeremy Tonet, who rates the stock as a Buy with a $178 price target, implying another ~30% upside from current levels that makes him the second-most bullish of the 15 analysts surveyed by FactSet who cover the company.

Tonet says Vistra (VST) stands out given its attractive exposure to the Electric Reliability Council of Texas, as well as its natural gas generation and nuclear optionality.

"While exact timing around nuclear contracting opportunities appears uncertain, we would not be surprised to see an announcement materialize over the next year, potentially a very positive catalyst for shares in our view," Tonet wrote this week.

The analyst's second-favorite IPP is Buy rated Talen Energy (TLN), setting a $268 PT, which is ~50% above current levels, followed by Constellation Energy (CEG).

"With no retail business, unlike IPP peers, [Talen] offers the highest leverage to PJM pricing trends and upside in a higher demand-growth outlook," and backed by nuclear production tax credits "offering an increasing floor to power prices, while retaining price upside, [Constellation Energy] carries industry-leading growth visibility through the end of the decade," according to Tonet.

data.title: Acadia Healthcare falls after report on new govt. probe
data.description:
Shares of Acadia Healthcare (NASDAQ:ACHC) traded lower on Friday after The New York Times reported that the Veterans Affairs Department is investigating the behavioral care provider for its alleged fraudulent activities related to government health insurance programs.

Citing three people familiar with the matter, the publication said that the agency’s inspector general is conducting an investigation into Acadia’s (NASDAQ:ACHC) alleged practice of keeping patients for a period longer than medically necessary.

Two people said that the Veterans Affairs authority is investigating whether Acadia (ACHC) fraudulently billed government insurance programs for patients who were stable enough to be discharged and did not require intensive inpatient care.

The Veterans Affairs Department did not immediately respond to requests for comment. Franklin, Tennessee-based healthcare provider, recently disclosed that it has come under the scrutiny of several other federal investigators and was awaiting potential inquiries from the Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC).

data.title: VanEck Gold Miners ETF set for seven straight sessions of gains
data.description:
Shares of VanEck Gold Miners ETF (NYSEARCA:GDX) were on track for their seventh straight session of gains on Friday, as the stock rose 4.29% to $43.26 in afternoon trade.

The fund, which invests a huge chunk of its assets in stocks of companies involved in the gold mining industry, gained 4.06% in the preceding six trading sessions. The stock has gained more than 35% so far this year, compared to an over 22% rise in the broader S&P 500 Index.

GDX is up 9% over the past one month. The stock closed 1.20% higher on Thursday, at $41.49.

Gold futures have added to record highs recently as investors seek out safe-haven assets in fears of the ongoing war in the Middle East and the implication of the upcoming U.S. Presidential elections for economic and trade policies.

Even though gold has gained more than 30% YTD, making it one of the best performing commodities of 2024, some analysts believe gold is not likely to reach a price ceiling anytime soon.

Seeking Alpha’s Quant rating considers the stock a BUY with an A+ in momentum and liquidity, and a D- on risk.

Meanwhile, Seeking Alpha analysts remain cautious regarding the stock and have rated it as a HOLD.

Analyst Josh Arnold believes that the rally in gold and gold miners, particularly the VanEck Gold Miners ETF, looks exhausted and may be due for a breather.

“Seasonality trends indicate a historically weak November for gold, with better prospects in December and January, suggesting a potential consolidation period,” Arnold said, adding that he feels that the rally looks tired and as though it may be about to roll over.

data.title: Bitcoin set to rise W/W buoyed by inflows in bitcoin ETFs as presidential election nears
data.description:
Bitcoin (BTC-USD) is expected to rise 10.4% on a weekly basis, helped by strong inflows into spot bitcoin exchange-trade funds, as volatility increases in the run-up to the U.S. Presidential Election.

The cryptocurrency started the week strongly, and breached the $65K mark on Monday, with a sizable jump on the heels of China's latest economic stimulus plan that offered scant details.

BTC then surpassed $67K on Tuesday for the first time in more than two months.

The U.S. presidential race is another catalyst propping up crypto prices. In the past few days, prediction markets have shifted, now giving pro-crypto Republican candidate Donald Trump better odds of winning than his Democratic opponent, Vice President Kamala Harris.

"A continued rebound in Trump's election odds plus positive macro sentiment continues to buoy crypto price action. BTC ETF has seen 5 consecutive days of positive inflows with Wednesday & Thursday both seeing over 450M of net buying," said Augustine Fan, Head of Insights, SOFA.org.

In an eye-catching move, $559.9M flowed into 11 spot bitcoin ETFs on Monday, the largest amount since early June, according to data from London-based Farside Investors. BlackRock's iShares Bitcoin Trust ETF (IBIT), the largest bitcoin fund, drew in $79.5M of that total.

"Bitcoin and Ethereum ETF inflows have jumped in the last week, which likely caused their prices to jump, and we haven't seen any macro factors that could've caused this," said Jeff Mei, COO at crypto exchange, BTSE.

"The inflows are almost certainly due to euphoria leading up to the U.S. Presidential Election, as historically, volatility increases by about 20% in the time frame leading up to and after a Presidential election," Mei added.

Notable News

  • 25-year-old Eric Council Jr. was arrested, and charged with allegedly hacking the U.S. SEC's X account in January and posting a fake post regarding bitcoin (BTC-USD) exchange-traded funds, according to the Department of Justice.
  • Deputy Finance Minister Maurizio Leo said that Italy plans to hike capital gains tax on cryptocurrencies like Bitcoin (BTC-USD) to 42% from 26%.
  • Elon Musk's Tesla (TSLA) has reportedly transferred almost all $760M of bitcoins (BTC-USD) that it owns to unidentified wallets.
  • World Liberty Financial, the cryptocurrency project backed by U.S. Republican presidential candidate Donald Trump and his family, started with some hiccups, as the website suffered numerous outages after going live and the public sale of the new WLFI token trailed the $300M fundraising goal by a wide margin.

Bitcoin, Ether prices

Bitcoin (BTC-USD) rose 3% to ~$69K, as it hovered near the $70K mark at 3:16 pm on Friday. Ether (ETH-USD) up 2.9% at $2.7K.

Bitcoin’s price surged to levels not seen since July, fueled by positive inflows and optimism around a potential bullish breakout, said SA analyst Dean Popplewell, adding, "Technical analysis shows Bitcoin facing resistance at various levels, with support levels identified in case of a price decline."

data.title: Zeta Global falls as Barclays downgrades on challenging growth outlook
data.description:
Zeta Global (NYSE:ZETA) shares dropped 8% after Barclays downgraded the stock to "Equal Weight" from "Overweight," maintaining a $28 price target.

Barclays noted that Zeta's outlook is more challenging as investors focus on FY25 growth prospects, weighing choices between improving margins or pursuing additional revenue growth. The brokerage also views the current valuation as less appealing due to a lower assigned multiple for integrated revenue growth.

Barclays expects Zeta's agency business to generate about $130M in revenue by the end of FY24. However, they caution that the investment case could get tougher in FY25 as Zeta faces difficult year-over-year comparisons, especially after recent growth driven by agency partnerships and improved EBITDA margins.

data.title: Humana goes positive amid takeover speculation
data.description:
• Humana (NYSE:HUM) quickly erased its losses and went positive amid takeover speculation.
  • Humana (HUM) is speculated to have received a takeover bid, according to a Betaville "uncooked" alert that was circulating among traders on Friday.
  • Cigna (CI) and a Western European company may be interested, according to the report.
  • Developing story ...

data.title: Atlanta Fed's Bostic: 'I'm not in a rush' to get to interest rates down to neutral
data.description:
“I’m not in a rush to get to neutral,” Federal Reserve Bank of Atlanta President Raphael Bostic said of interest rates during a moderated conversation at an event hosted by the Mississippi Council on Economic Education in Jackson, Mississippi.

He noted the Fed's policy rate is still "a ways" above neutral, which is the rate at which monetary policy neither stimulates nor restrains economic growth. He estimates the neutral rate stands at 3.0%-3.5%

“We must get inflation back to our 2% target, and I don’t want us to get to a place where inflation stalls out because we haven’t been restrictive for long enough. So I’m going to be patient,” the Atlanta Fed chief said.

Bostic expects inflation to fall to the central bank's 2% target by the end of 2025, he added.

Last week, Bostic said he was "definitely open" to holding rates steady at the central bank's November gathering, as disinflation appeared to stall with the latest CPI data.

data.title: Astellas wins FDA nod for first-line gastric cancer therapy
data.description:
• Astellas Pharma (OTCPK:ALPMF) announced on Friday that the U.S. FDA approved its antibody therapy Vyloy as a first-line option for certain adults with gastric or gastroesophageal junction (GEJ) adenocarcinoma as part of a combination regimen.
  • Vyloy is a targeted cancer therapy aimed at the tumor biomarker claudin (CLDN) 18.2. The approval allows its use for claudin (CLDN) 18.2 positive tumors detected by an FDA-approved test such as Roche’s (OTCQX:RHHBY) (OTCQX:RHHBF) Ventana CLDN18 (43-14A) RxDx Assay.
  • Vyloy will specifically be indicated in combination with fluoropyrimidine- and platinum-containing chemotherapy for human epidermal growth factor receptor 2 (HER2)-negative, CLDN 18.2-positive gastric or GEJ adenocarcinoma.
  • The approval is backed by Astellas’ (OTCPK:ALPMY) Phase 3 SPOTLIGHT and GLOW clinical trials, which met their primary endpoint of progression-free survival and overall survival, a key secondary endpoint.

data.title: Catalyst Watch: Earnings blitz, Fed speakers, and notable healthcare conferences
data.description:
Welcome to Seeking Alpha's Catalyst Watch - a breakdown of some of next week's actionable events that stand out. Read more about the events that may impact stock prices next week.

Monday - October 21

  • Volatility watch - Options trading volume is elevated on Alto Neuroscience (ANRO) and NuScale Power (SMR). The most overbought stocks per their 14-day relative strength index include Arcadium Lithium (ALTM), Eton Pharmaceuticals (ETON), and Cal-Maine (CALM). The most oversold stocks per their 14-day Relative Strength Index include Bioceres Crop Solutions (BIOX), Stride (LRN), LiveOne (LVO). Short interest is elevated on Wheeler Real Estate Investment (WHLR) and Hertz Global Holdings (HTZ) as earnings approach.
  • Earnings watch - Notable companies due to report include Nucor (NUE) and Logitech (LOGI). Options trading implies a double-digit share price swing for Medpace (MEDP) after its report.
  • All day - Notable investor event includes Jefferies' (JEF) Investor Day.
  • All day - Qualcomm (QCOM) will hold its Snapdragon Summit in Maui.
  • All day - It is the FDA action date for Camurus' (OTCPK:CAMRF) Octreotide (CAM2029) new drug application.
  • 1:00 p.m. Minneapolis Federal Reserve Bank President Neel Kashkari will participate in Chippewa Falls Chamber of Commerce town hall.
  • 3:05 p.m. Kansas City Federal Reserve Bank President Jeffrey Schmid will speak on the economic and monetary policy outlook before a hybrid event hosted by the Chartered Financial Analyst Society, Kansas City.

Tuesday - October 22

  • Earnings watch - Notable companies due to report include General Electric (GE), Philip Morris (PM), Verizon (VZ), Texas Instruments (TXN), Lockheed Martin (LMT), General Motors (GM), and 3M (MMM). Options trading implies a sizable swing in share price for Denny's (DENN) and Stride (LRN) following their reports.
  • All day - WTI crude November futures will expire. Crude oil futures have seen extra volatility over the last year on contract expiration dates.
  • All day - The 13D Monitor Active-Passive Investor Summit could include stock picks and activist target reveals from notable participating hedge fund firms Starboard Value, Impactive Capital, Corvex Management, Sachem Head Capital Management, Engaged Capital, JANA Partners, and others.
  • All day - McCormick & Company (MKC) will hold an Investor Day event, with members of the executive leadership team planning to discuss the company's long-term strategy and financial outlook.
  • 10:00 a.m. Philadelphia Federal Reserve Bank President Patrick Harker will speak on fintech before the Eighth Annual Fintech Conference hosted by the Federal Reserve Bank of Philadelphia.

Wednesday - October 23

  • Earnings watch - Notable companies due to report include Tesla (TSLA), Coca-Cola (KO), T-Mobile (TMUS), IBM (IBM), AT&T (T), and Boeing (BA). Options trading implies a sizable swing in share price for Teradyne (TER) and Winnebago (WGO) following their reports.
  • All day - The healthcare sector will be in the spotlight, with the start of American Society of Nephrology Kidney Week and the AACR Symposium on Molecular Targets and Cancer Therapeutics.
  • All day - The Federal Reserve will release its Beige Book Report. The report will summarize current economic conditions across the Fed's districts.
  • All day - Albertsons (ACI)/Kroger (KR) - Closing arguments in Washington state trial over deal.
  • 8:00 a.m. - Boston Scientific (BSX) will hold Q3 earnings call and investors will see if the company gives any updates on the Axonics (AXNX) deal.
  • 5:30 p.m. Tesla (TSLA) will hold its earnings conference call. A point of major emphasis will be Elon Musk's commentary on the timing of a new mass-market cheaper electric vehicle, as well as the latest updates on FSD, energy storage business, and robotaxi developments. EV stocks with the tightest trading correlation to Tesla (TSLA) after earnings include Rivian Automotive (RIVN), Faraday Future Intelligent (FFIE), and NIO (NIO). Meanwhile, Uber (UBER) has had a negative trading correlation to Tesla (TSLA) on days with robotaxi developments.

Thursday - October 24

  • Earnings watch - Notable companies due to report include Valero (VLO), UPS (UPS), Honeywell (HON), American Airlines (AAL), Northrop Grumman (NOC), Southwest Airlines (LUV), and Dow (DOW).
  • All day - Notable investor events include Beyond's (BYON) Investor Meeting. The meeting will take place after the company entered into a partnership with The Container Store (TCS) aimed at improving customer experience utilizing both the Bed Bath and Beyond Brand and Container Store brands.
  • 8:30 a.m. The September Durable Goods Order will be released.
  • All day - Albertsons (ACI)/Kroger (KR) - Closing arguments in Colorado state trial over deal.

Friday - October 25

  • Earnings watch - Notable companies due to report include Centene (CNC), HCA (HCA), and Colgate-Palmolive (CL).
  • All day - The two-day DareTable event will begin in New York City. Analysts have pointed to Asana (ASAN), Monday.com (MNDY), and Atlassian (TEAM) as companies that could generate some buzz.
